segments.text: Sarai, Abram's wife, hadn't been able to have any children for him. However, she owned a female Egyptian slave named Hagar,
so Sarai said to Abram,
Please listen to me. The Lord hasn't let me have any children. So please go and sleep with my slave. Maybe I can have a family through her.
segments.speaker: Sarai

segments.text: Abram agreed to what Sarai had suggested.
So Sarai, Abram's wife took her Egyptian slave Hagar and gave her to her husband as his wife. Abram had been living in the land of Canaan for ten years when this happened.
Abram slept with Hagar and she became pregnant. When she realized she was pregnant, she treated her mistress with contempt.
Then Sarai complained to Abram,
What I'm suffering is all your doing! I gave you my servant to sleep with, and now that she knows she's pregnant, she treats me with contempt. May the Lord decide who's at fault—you or me!
segments.speaker: Sarai

segments.text: Listen, she's your slave!
segments.speaker: Abram

segments.text: Abram replied.
You can do whatever you want to her.
segments.speaker: Abram

segments.text: Sarai treated Hagar so badly that she ran away.
The angel of the Lord met Hagar at a spring in the desert—the spring on the road to Shur.
He asked her,
Hagar, Sarai's slave—where have you come from, and where are you going?
segments.speaker: Angel of the LORD

segments.text: I'm running away from my mistress Sarai,
segments.speaker: Hagar

segments.text: she replied.
Go back to your mistress and do what she tells you,
segments.speaker: the Angel of the Lord

segments.text: the angel of the Lord told her.
Then he continued,
I will give you many, many descendants—in fact they'll be so many they can't be counted.
segments.speaker: Angel of the LORD

segments.text: The angel of the Lord went on to tell her:
Listen! You're pregnant, and you will have a son. You are to name him Ishmael,  for the Lord has heard how you've suffered.
segments.speaker: Angel of the LORD

segments.text: He'll be a wild donkey kind of man—he will fight with everyone, and everyone will fight with him. He will forever be fighting with his relatives.
segments.speaker: the Angel of the Lord

segments.text: From then on Hagar called the Lord who spoke to her,
You are the God who sees me,
segments.speaker: Hagar

segments.text: because she said,
Here I saw the one who sees me.
segments.speaker: Hagar

segments.text: That's why the well is called
the Well of the Living One who Sees Me.
segments.speaker: That's why the wellis

segments.text: It's still there, between Kadesh and Bered.
Hagar gave birth to a son for Abram. Abram named his son Ishmael.
Abram was 86 when Hagar had Ishmael.
